heroHeadline: Sheinbaum at 568 Days: MLB Mexico City Series Opens; Screwworm Cattle Crisis Deepens; Sheinbaum-Carney CUSMA Call; Lazzeri Nominated as New U.S. Ambassador
heroSubtitle: On April 25 — a Saturday without a mañanera — Mexico's diplomatic and economic agenda advanced on multiple fronts. The 2026 MLB Mexico City Series opened at Estadio Alfredo Harp Helú with Game 1 (Arizona Diamondbacks vs San Diego Padres), one day after Sheinbaum hosted both teams at the National Palace to promote Mexico's sports tourism profile ahead of the FIFA World Cup 2026. On April 24, Prime Minister Mark Carney and President Sheinbaum held a bilateral call to coordinate CUSMA positions, welcoming Economy Secretary Ebrard's upcoming trade mission to Canada and noting that the Team Canada Mission earlier in 2026 produced 20+ new commercial agreements; the first official US-Mexico USMCA negotiating round is set for the week of May 25 in Mexico City. Separately, Sheinbaum nominated economist Roberto Lazzeri Montaño — head of Nafin-Bancomext and former chief of staff to Finance Secretary Ramírez de la O — as Mexico's new U.S. ambassador, prioritizing technical USMCA expertise over political profile. The screwworm crisis intensified with USDA data confirming 700+ new cases in Mexico during April, keeping the US livestock import ban in force; Sheinbaum expressed confidence that bilateral cooperation — including a US-supported sterile fly plant in Chiapas due in June — would restore cattle trade. Looking ahead to April 26: the 'Tren Felipe Ángeles' AIFA express inaugurates with promotional fares of 45 pesos for the full Buenavista–AIFA journey, a key World Cup infrastructure milestone.
footerNote: Data sourced from Diario Oficial, INEGI, Banco de México, SNSP, Secretaría de Economía, INAH, Reuters, AP, El Universal, Reforma, Animal Político, Mexico News Daily, Infobae, Milenio, La Jornada, Al Jazeera, Bloomberg, AtlasIntel, USTR, Chatham House, Mexico Business News, El Financiero, La Silla Rota, Proceso, CNN Español, Lopez Doriga, The Yucatan Times, Excelsior, La Razón de México, Quinto Poder, PBS NewsHour, NBC News, Time, NPR, CBC, Japan Kantei, Japan MOFA, SDP Noticias, Eje Central, TVAzteca, UnoTV, CBS News, El Mañana de Nuevo Laredo, Bloomberg Línea, Canadian PMO, UPI, MLB.com, El Imparcial
